summary: ACCEPT. R2 review confirms the prior P1 typecheck regressions are fixed and the M2 host-hash canary satisfies the task acceptance criteria. apps/web typecheck now passes, apps/web has no direct package.json or lockfile importer dependency on @monsoon/sim-core, the canary page still creates a real module Dedicated Worker, and fresh Chromium plus packaged Electron host canary runs match the Node baseline for hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, and save byte length 10920. Electron security posture remains narrow: no new IPC, preload authority, Node integration, filesystem authority, remote URL loading, or webSecurity weakening was found.

tests_run.result: On test/m2-host-hash-canary. Dirty paths are the intended canary app files, lead-owned orchestration/task files, route files, and handoff files; no forbidden task paths appeared in git name-status.
Tracked source diffs are in apps/desktop, apps/sim-runner, apps/web, and allowed project orchestration/task files. Untracked canary source, routes, and handoffs are under allowed paths.
No diff remains in apps/web/package.json or pnpm-lock.yaml; the prior direct web package dependency on @monsoon/sim-core is removed.
tsc --noEmit --project tsconfig.json passed. Prior TS4111, TS2304, and TS5097 failures are fixed.
ESLint passed for apps/web source and vite.config.ts.
Node and Worker-compatible hashes matched: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save/load byte length 10920.
Vite built dist/index.html, dist/host-hash-canary.html, and the host-hash-canary worker bundle from current sources.

Electron security preferences passed; preload exposes only monsoonDesktop; renderer file navigation is restricted to the packaged renderer root; allowed IPC channels remain desktop:getRuntimeInfo and desktop:setFullscreen.
Packaged main, preload, renderer, and platform adapter artifacts to .tmp/desktop-package/MonsoonSovereigns-win32-x64.
Node baseline matched Chromium dedicated worker: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.
Node baseline matched Electron renderer dedicated worker: hello 61e0ba1c, command/query d275faa4, save/load 28074edd, save byte length 10920.
Writer R2 handoff validates against project/schemas/handoff.schema.json.
Task JSON shape ok: M2-HOST-HASH-CANARY-001 REVIEW 6 acceptance criteria and 10 required tests.

Confirmed no apps/web package/lockfile diff for @monsoon/sim-core, built canary asset contains new Worker(..., { type: module }), Electron main activates canary only with MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY=1, and security settings remain nodeIntegration=false, contextIsolation=true, sandbox=true, webSecurity=true.

decisions: Accept M2-HOST-HASH-CANARY-001 after R2 because all task acceptance criteria have reproducible reviewer evidence.
The previous P1 findings are closed: strict web typecheck passes, Dedicated Worker typing no longer fails, and no direct apps/web manifest or lockfile dependency on @monsoon/sim-core remains.
The Chromium canary is a real browser page-created module Dedicated Worker and compares its hashes to a Node baseline printed by the runner.
The Electron canary launches the packaged Electron app with MONSOON_HOST_HASH_CANARY=1 and compares the packaged renderer Dedicated Worker result to the same Node baseline.

Electron security posture is unchanged in reviewed source and validated build output: IPC allowlist and preload bridge remain narrow, Node integration remains disabled, context isolation and sandbox remain enabled, remote URL loading was not introduced.
risks: apps/desktop/scripts/copy-renderer.mjs now expects apps/web/dist to exist before desktop packaging; the required review sequence ran pnpm build:web before desktop:package.

apps/web/src/worker/hello-simulation-adapter.mjs imports sim-core source through the existing worker-compatible adapter path; reviewer accepts this as current project-local canary/test plumbing because web typecheck and build pass and no package dependency or lockfile change remains.
